skill.name: meta-screening-fulltext
skill.description: Screen full-text papers against inclusion/exclusion criteria, with optional PubMed metadata check using PMID. Use when the user needs to evaluate a paper for a meta-analysis.
skill.summary: Screen full-text papers against inclusion/exclusion criteria, with optional PubMed metadata check using PMID. Use when the user needs to evaluate a paper for a meta-analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 46 static analysis findings were evaluated in context. 45 are false positives caused by pattern confusion: Markdown code fences (backtick execution), PICO medical framework acronyms (weak crypto), paper screening terminology (screen capture), and workflow instruction text (system reconnaissance). One true positive was confirmed: a file write operation in scripts/extract_pdf.py:53 which is the legitimate core function of the PDF text extraction tool. The skill has no malicious intent, no obfuscated code, no credential harvesting, and no unauthorized network calls. Previous audit graded this skill Production Ready (85/100) with all assertions passing.

low_findings.title: File write operation in PDF extraction script
low_findings.description: scripts/extract_pdf.py uses open() with write mode to save extracted PDF text to disk. This is the legitimate core function of the script. The output file path is user-controlled via --output argument or auto-generated from the input filename. No path traversal risk because the script uses pathlib for safe path handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: File write operation via open()
dangerous_patterns.description: Standard Python file write operation in PDF text extraction script. Writes extracted text content to a user-specified output file. This is expected behavior for the script's documented purpose.

content.user_title: Screen full-text papers for systematic review inclusion
content.value_statement: Systematic reviewers spend hours manually screening full-text papers against inclusion criteria. This skill automates the process by evaluating full-text papers against PICO-based criteria and optionally fetching PubMed metadata for faster screening decisions.
actual_capabilities: Screen full-text papers against user-provided inclusion and exclusion criteria
Fetch paper title and abstract from PubMed using a PMID for metadata verification
Generate structured JSON output with Include/Exclude result and standardized exclusion reasons
Extract text from PDF files using the bundled Python extraction script
Apply PICO framework analysis for evidence-based screening decisions
Use standardized exclusion terminology: wrong population, intervention, comparator, outcomes, or study design
limitations: Cannot access PubMed without a valid PMID; requires the user to provide the identifier
Does not support batch screening of multiple papers in a single run
PDF text extraction quality depends on the source PDF file format and encoding
Screening accuracy relies on clearly defined inclusion and exclusion criteria from the user

use_cases.title: Full-text screening for meta-analysis
use_cases.description: A researcher screening the final set of full-text papers for a meta-analysis uses this skill to evaluate each paper against their PICO criteria. The skill produces a structured Include/Exclude decision with a standardized reason.

use_cases.title: PubMed metadata verification
use_cases.description: A librarian or information specialist verifies paper eligibility by cross-referencing PubMed metadata (title, abstract) with inclusion criteria before retrieving the full text. This saves time when full-text access is limited.

use_cases.title: PDF text extraction for review
use_cases.description: A research assistant has a folder of PDF papers from database searches. They use the bundled extraction script to convert PDFs to plain text, then run screening on the extracted text against the study protocol criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Basic paper screening
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen this paper against my criteria. Here is the full text: [paste paper text]. Inclusion criteria: [list criteria]. Exclusion criteria: [list criteria].
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has paper text available and needs a quick screening decision.

prompt_templates.title: PubMed-based screening
prompt_templates.prompt: Check PMID [enter PMID] on PubMed and screen the paper. Inclusion criteria: [list]. Exclusion criteria: [list]. Use the PubMed metadata to make your decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a PMID and wants to screen using PubMed metadata before retrieving full text.

prompt_templates.title: PDF file screening workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from the PDF file at [path/to/paper.pdf] and screen it against these criteria. Inclusion criteria: [list]. Exclusion criteria: [list].
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a PDF file and needs end-to-end extraction and screening.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed PICO criteria evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen this paper with detailed PICO analysis. Population: [description]. Intervention: [description]. Comparator: [description]. Outcome: [description]. Study design: [type]. Full text: [paste text]. Provide Include/Exclude with reasoning mapped to each PICO element.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user conducting a complex meta-analysis needs criteria mapped to each PICO component.

output_examples.input: Screen a paper about statin use in elderly patients with heart disease.
output_examples.output: The skill returns an Include decision with explanation that the population (elderly with heart disease), intervention (statin therapy), and outcomes (cardiac events) all match the specified criteria. Exclusion reason is marked as NA.

output_examples.input: Screen a paper that describes a narrative review rather than a clinical trial.
output_examples.output: The skill returns an Exclude decision with reason set to 'Wrong study design', noting the publication is a narrative review which does not meet the required study design criteria.

best_practices: Define inclusion and exclusion criteria using PICO format before running the skill for consistent results
Always verify the extracted PDF text quality before screening, especially with scanned documents or non-standard formatting
Review the screening decision and reasoning before relying on it for a published systematic review
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to screen papers outside your declared research protocol criteria, as this introduces selection bias
Do not skip the PMID verification step when a PMID is available, as PubMed metadata provides a reliable second source of information
Do not treat the skill output as a replacement for duplicate independent screening by two reviewers, which is the gold standard in systematic reviews

faq.question: Does this skill work without a PMID?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide the full paper text directly and the skill will screen it against your criteria without PubMed metadata.

faq.question: What exclusion categories does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill uses five standardized categories: Wrong population, Wrong intervention, Wrong comparator, Wrong outcomes, and Wrong study design.

faq.question: Can this skill screen multiple papers at once?
faq.answer: No. The skill processes one paper at a time. You need to run it separately for each paper you want to evaluate.

faq.question: How does the PDF extraction script work?
faq.answer: The script uses PyPDF2 to read PDF files and extract text content. It saves the output as a plain text file with page markers.

faq.question: What is the PICO framework used in this skill?
faq.answer: PICO stands for Population, Intervention, Comparator, and Outcome. It is a standard framework used in evidence-based medicine to structure clinical questions.

faq.question: Is the screening output reproducible?
faq.answer: The skill produces structured JSON output with Include/Exclude and reasoning. Results depend on the quality of your criteria and paper text.
